APP_DOMAIN.name: APP_DOMAIN
APP_DOMAIN.consumerProof: Canonical public host identity that later derives APP_URL, certificate defaults, and server names for the application path.
APP_DOMAIN.ownerProof: The operator owns the public host identity for the supported deployment contract.

APP_NAME.name: APP_NAME
APP_NAME.consumerProof: Laravel config/app.php consumes APP_NAME for the application display name and the session/cache naming defaults.
APP_NAME.ownerProof: The project keeps APP_NAME as a compatibility-friendly app label while the current template still exposes it.

APP_ENV.name: APP_ENV
APP_ENV.consumerProof: Laravel config/app.php consumes APP_ENV to select the runtime environment mode.
APP_ENV.ownerProof: Bootstrap owns the normal lab/demo default for APP_ENV even though the template still shows the compatibility variable.

APP_KEY.name: APP_KEY
APP_KEY.consumerProof: Laravel config/app.php consumes APP_KEY as the encryption key for the application cipher.
APP_KEY.ownerProof: Bootstrap owns first-run APP_KEY generation for bundled lab/demo flows, while production profiles must inject or validate the secret outside the normal template path.

APP_DEBUG.name: APP_DEBUG
APP_DEBUG.consumerProof: Laravel config/app.php consumes APP_DEBUG to enable or disable detailed exception output.
APP_DEBUG.ownerProof: Bootstrap owns the normal mode default for APP_DEBUG while compatibility keeps the current template entry.

APP_URL.name: APP_URL
APP_URL.consumerProof: Laravel still consumes APP_URL, but the cleanup contract derives it from APP_DOMAIN instead of treating it as a separate operator input.
APP_URL.ownerProof: The operator does not own a second public URL input in the normal path once APP_DOMAIN is canonical.

APP_LOCALE.name: APP_LOCALE
APP_LOCALE.consumerProof: Laravel config/app.php consumes APP_LOCALE as the primary translation locale.
APP_LOCALE.ownerProof: The project currently allows APP_LOCALE as an application policy override while the compatibility template still exposes it.

APP_FALLBACK_LOCALE.name: APP_FALLBACK_LOCALE
APP_FALLBACK_LOCALE.consumerProof: Laravel config/app.php consumes APP_FALLBACK_LOCALE for translation fallback resolution.
APP_FALLBACK_LOCALE.ownerProof: The project currently keeps APP_FALLBACK_LOCALE as a documented compatibility setting for localization policy.

APP_FAKER_LOCALE.name: APP_FAKER_LOCALE
APP_FAKER_LOCALE.consumerProof: Laravel config/app.php consumes APP_FAKER_LOCALE for faker-generated data defaults.
APP_FAKER_LOCALE.ownerProof: The template still carries APP_FAKER_LOCALE as a developer-facing compatibility setting for seeded/test data generation.

APP_MAINTENANCE_DRIVER.name: APP_MAINTENANCE_DRIVER
APP_MAINTENANCE_DRIVER.consumerProof: Laravel config/app.php consumes APP_MAINTENANCE_DRIVER for maintenance mode storage selection.
APP_MAINTENANCE_DRIVER.ownerProof: The current template still exposes APP_MAINTENANCE_DRIVER as a compatibility knob for maintenance-mode behavior.

TRUSTED_PROXIES.name: TRUSTED_PROXIES
TRUSTED_PROXIES.consumerProof: Laravel config/app.php consumes TRUSTED_PROXIES to build the allowlist of reverse proxies allowed to supply forwarded headers.
TRUSTED_PROXIES.ownerProof: The compatibility contract still documents TRUSTED_PROXIES as the override point for narrow proxy trust policy.

TRUSTED_PROXY_HEADERS.name: TRUSTED_PROXY_HEADERS
TRUSTED_PROXY_HEADERS.consumerProof: Laravel config/app.php consumes TRUSTED_PROXY_HEADERS to decide which forwarded header profile is allowed.
TRUSTED_PROXY_HEADERS.ownerProof: The current template still exposes TRUSTED_PROXY_HEADERS as the compatibility override for reverse-proxy header policy.

LOG_CHANNEL.name: LOG_CHANNEL
LOG_CHANNEL.consumerProof: Laravel config/logging.php consumes LOG_CHANNEL to select the default logging channel.
LOG_CHANNEL.ownerProof: The compatibility template still lists LOG_CHANNEL as the primary override for the default Laravel logging sink.

LOG_STACK.name: LOG_STACK
LOG_STACK.consumerProof: Laravel config/logging.php consumes LOG_STACK to expand the stacked logging channels list.
LOG_STACK.ownerProof: The current template preserves LOG_STACK as the compatibility override for stacked logging composition.

LOG_DEPRECATIONS_CHANNEL.name: LOG_DEPRECATIONS_CHANNEL
LOG_DEPRECATIONS_CHANNEL.consumerProof: Laravel config/logging.php consumes LOG_DEPRECATIONS_CHANNEL for the deprecation warnings channel.
LOG_DEPRECATIONS_CHANNEL.ownerProof: The compatibility template still lists LOG_DEPRECATIONS_CHANNEL as the override for deprecation logging.

LOG_LEVEL.name: LOG_LEVEL
LOG_LEVEL.consumerProof: Laravel config/logging.php consumes LOG_LEVEL for the default severity thresholds across several log channels.
LOG_LEVEL.ownerProof: Bootstrap owns the normal log-level defaults, while the template still keeps LOG_LEVEL as a compatibility override.

HTTP_LOGGING_ENABLED.name: HTTP_LOGGING_ENABLED
HTTP_LOGGING_ENABLED.consumerProof: Laravel config/http_logging.php consumes HTTP_LOGGING_ENABLED to enable or disable response logging.
HTTP_LOGGING_ENABLED.ownerProof: The project still keeps HTTP_LOGGING_ENABLED as a documented application logging policy override.

HTTP_LOGGING_SUCCESS.name: HTTP_LOGGING_SUCCESS
HTTP_LOGGING_SUCCESS.consumerProof: Laravel config/http_logging.php consumes HTTP_LOGGING_SUCCESS to decide whether 2xx responses are logged.
HTTP_LOGGING_SUCCESS.ownerProof: The template still exposes HTTP_LOGGING_SUCCESS as a compatibility toggle for response logging noise.

HTTP_LOGGING_REDIRECTS.name: HTTP_LOGGING_REDIRECTS
HTTP_LOGGING_REDIRECTS.consumerProof: Laravel config/http_logging.php consumes HTTP_LOGGING_REDIRECTS to decide whether 3xx responses are logged.
HTTP_LOGGING_REDIRECTS.ownerProof: The compatibility template still lists HTTP_LOGGING_REDIRECTS as an application logging policy override.

HTTP_LOGGING_SLOW_THRESHOLD.name: HTTP_LOGGING_SLOW_THRESHOLD
HTTP_LOGGING_SLOW_THRESHOLD.consumerProof: Laravel config/http_logging.php consumes HTTP_LOGGING_SLOW_THRESHOLD for the slow-request warning threshold.
HTTP_LOGGING_SLOW_THRESHOLD.ownerProof: The compatibility template still exposes HTTP_LOGGING_SLOW_THRESHOLD as an application logging threshold override.

DB_CONNECTION.name: DB_CONNECTION
DB_CONNECTION.consumerProof: Laravel config/database.php consumes DB_CONNECTION to select the default database driver.
DB_CONNECTION.ownerProof: The current template still keeps DB_CONNECTION as the compatibility selector for the main Laravel database connection.

DB_HOST.name: DB_HOST
DB_HOST.consumerProof: Laravel config/database.php consumes DB_HOST for the bundled Postgres connection hostname.
DB_HOST.ownerProof: The template still exposes DB_HOST as the compatibility override for the default database host.

DB_PORT.name: DB_PORT
DB_PORT.consumerProof: Laravel config/database.php consumes DB_PORT for the bundled Postgres connection port.
DB_PORT.ownerProof: The template still exposes DB_PORT as the compatibility override for the default database port.

DB_DATABASE.name: DB_DATABASE
DB_DATABASE.consumerProof: Laravel config/database.php consumes DB_DATABASE for the bundled Postgres database name.
DB_DATABASE.ownerProof: The compatibility template still lists DB_DATABASE as the current application database-name override.

DB_USERNAME.name: DB_USERNAME
DB_USERNAME.consumerProof: Laravel config/database.php consumes DB_USERNAME for the bundled Postgres connection username.
DB_USERNAME.ownerProof: The compatibility template still keeps DB_USERNAME as the current application database-user override.

DB_PASSWORD.name: DB_PASSWORD
DB_PASSWORD.consumerProof: Laravel config/database.php consumes DB_PASSWORD for the application database password.
DB_PASSWORD.ownerProof: Bootstrap owns the bundled lab/demo database-secret generation path, while production or external database profiles must inject or validate DB_PASSWORD outside the normal template contract.

REDIS_CLIENT.name: REDIS_CLIENT
REDIS_CLIENT.consumerProof: Laravel config/database.php consumes REDIS_CLIENT to choose the Redis PHP client implementation.
REDIS_CLIENT.ownerProof: The compatibility template still exposes REDIS_CLIENT as the current Redis client override.

REDIS_HOST.name: REDIS_HOST
REDIS_HOST.consumerProof: Laravel config/database.php consumes REDIS_HOST for the default Redis host.
REDIS_HOST.ownerProof: The template still keeps REDIS_HOST as the compatibility override for the bundled Redis hostname.

REDIS_PASSWORD.name: REDIS_PASSWORD
REDIS_PASSWORD.consumerProof: Laravel config/database.php consumes REDIS_PASSWORD for Redis authentication when a secured Redis deployment is used.
REDIS_PASSWORD.ownerProof: Redis authentication belongs to an advanced secured-Redis profile, so the secret must be injected by that profile instead of staying in the normal bundled template.

REDIS_PORT.name: REDIS_PORT
REDIS_PORT.consumerProof: Laravel config/database.php consumes REDIS_PORT for the default Redis port.
REDIS_PORT.ownerProof: The template still keeps REDIS_PORT as the compatibility override for the bundled Redis port.

SESSION_DRIVER.name: SESSION_DRIVER
SESSION_DRIVER.consumerProof: Laravel config/session.php consumes SESSION_DRIVER to select the session backend.
SESSION_DRIVER.ownerProof: The compatibility template still exposes SESSION_DRIVER as the current application session backend override.

SESSION_LIFETIME.name: SESSION_LIFETIME
SESSION_LIFETIME.consumerProof: Laravel config/session.php consumes SESSION_LIFETIME for the idle timeout in minutes.
SESSION_LIFETIME.ownerProof: The compatibility template still keeps SESSION_LIFETIME as the session policy override.

SESSION_ENCRYPT.name: SESSION_ENCRYPT
SESSION_ENCRYPT.consumerProof: Laravel config/session.php consumes SESSION_ENCRYPT to decide whether session payloads are encrypted.
SESSION_ENCRYPT.ownerProof: The compatibility template still documents SESSION_ENCRYPT as the current session policy override.

SESSION_PATH.name: SESSION_PATH
SESSION_PATH.consumerProof: Laravel config/session.php consumes SESSION_PATH for the session cookie path scope.
SESSION_PATH.ownerProof: The current template still exposes SESSION_PATH as a compatibility override for cookie scoping.

SESSION_DOMAIN.name: SESSION_DOMAIN
SESSION_DOMAIN.consumerProof: Laravel config/session.php consumes SESSION_DOMAIN for the session cookie domain scope.
SESSION_DOMAIN.ownerProof: The compatibility template still exposes SESSION_DOMAIN as the current cookie-domain override.

SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE.name: SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE
SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE.consumerProof: Laravel config/session.php consumes SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE to decide whether the session cookie is HTTPS-only.
SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE.ownerProof: The current template still exposes SESSION_SECURE_COOKIE as the compatibility secure-cookie override.

FILESYSTEM_DISK.name: FILESYSTEM_DISK
FILESYSTEM_DISK.consumerProof: Laravel config/filesystems.php consumes FILESYSTEM_DISK to select the default filesystem disk.
FILESYSTEM_DISK.ownerProof: The compatibility template still keeps FILESYSTEM_DISK as the current Laravel storage-disk selector.

CACHE_STORE.name: CACHE_STORE
CACHE_STORE.consumerProof: Laravel config/cache.php consumes CACHE_STORE to select the default cache backend.
CACHE_STORE.ownerProof: The current template still exposes CACHE_STORE as the compatibility override for the default cache store.

MEMCACHED_HOST.name: MEMCACHED_HOST
MEMCACHED_HOST.consumerProof: Laravel config/cache.php still exposes MEMCACHED_HOST for the framework memcached store, but the bundled deployment does not ship Memcached as a supported service.
MEMCACHED_HOST.ownerProof: The project does not treat a Memcached host as part of the normal operator contract, so the current placeholder is only a removable framework carry-over.

QUEUE_CONNECTION.name: QUEUE_CONNECTION
QUEUE_CONNECTION.consumerProof: Laravel config/queue.php consumes QUEUE_CONNECTION to select the default queue backend.
QUEUE_CONNECTION.ownerProof: The compatibility template still keeps QUEUE_CONNECTION as the current queue backend selector.

MAIL_MAILER.name: MAIL_MAILER
MAIL_MAILER.consumerProof: Laravel config/mail.php consumes MAIL_MAILER to select the default mail transport.
MAIL_MAILER.ownerProof: The compatibility template still exposes MAIL_MAILER as the current Laravel mail transport selector.

MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS.name: MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS
MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS.consumerProof: Laravel config/mail.php consumes MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS for the global sender address.
MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS.ownerProof: The current template still keeps MAIL_FROM_ADDRESS as the compatibility sender-address override.

MAIL_FROM_NAME.name: MAIL_FROM_NAME
MAIL_FROM_NAME.consumerProof: Laravel config/mail.php consumes MAIL_FROM_NAME for the global sender display name.
MAIL_FROM_NAME.ownerProof: The current template still keeps MAIL_FROM_NAME as the compatibility sender-name override.

AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID.name: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID.consumerProof: Laravel config/filesystems.php consumes AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID for the optional S3 filesystem integration.
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID.ownerProof: AWS credentials belong to an external operator-managed integration and stay advanced-only rather than normal bundled deployment input.

AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.name: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.consumerProof: Laravel config/filesystems.php consumes AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY for the optional S3 filesystem integration.
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY.ownerProof: AWS credentials belong to an external operator-managed integration and stay advanced-only rather than normal bundled deployment input.

AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.name: AWS_DEFAULT_REGION
AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.consumerProof: Laravel config/filesystems.php consumes AWS_DEFAULT_REGION for the optional S3 filesystem integration.
AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.ownerProof: AWS region selection belongs to an external operator-managed integration and stays advanced-only in the current cleanup contract.

AWS_BUCKET.name: AWS_BUCKET
AWS_BUCKET.consumerProof: Laravel config/filesystems.php consumes AWS_BUCKET for the optional S3 filesystem integration.
AWS_BUCKET.ownerProof: The target S3 bucket is an external operator-managed integration detail and stays advanced-only in the current cleanup contract.

AWS_USE_PATH_STYLE_ENDPOINT.name: AWS_USE_PATH_STYLE_ENDPOINT
AWS_USE_PATH_STYLE_ENDPOINT.consumerProof: Laravel config/filesystems.php consumes AWS_USE_PATH_STYLE_ENDPOINT for optional S3-compatible storage integrations.
AWS_USE_PATH_STYLE_ENDPOINT.ownerProof: This flag only matters for advanced object-storage integrations and does not belong in the normal bundled deployment operator path.

CANONICAL_AUDIT_AUTH_SERVICE_SECRET.name: CANONICAL_AUDIT_AUTH_SERVICE_SECRET
CANONICAL_AUDIT_AUTH_SERVICE_SECRET.consumerProof: Laravel config/canonical_audit_ingestion.php consumes CANONICAL_AUDIT_AUTH_SERVICE_SECRET for auth-service request verification.
CANONICAL_AUDIT_AUTH_SERVICE_SECRET.ownerProof: Bootstrap owns the bundled shared-secret generation path for lab/demo, while production or external auth-service profiles must inject the secret outside the normal template contract.

BROADCAST_CONNECTION.name: BROADCAST_CONNECTION
BROADCAST_CONNECTION.consumerProof: The cleanup inventory found no real application, bootstrap, Compose, deployment, or supported documentation consumer for BROADCAST_CONNECTION in this project.
BROADCAST_CONNECTION.ownerProof: The project does not treat a broadcast driver as part of the normal operator contract, so the current placeholder is only a removable framework carry-over.

VITE_APP_NAME.name: VITE_APP_NAME
VITE_APP_NAME.consumerProof: The cleanup inventory found no real Vite, frontend, bootstrap, Compose, deployment, or supported documentation consumer for VITE_APP_NAME in this project.
VITE_APP_NAME.ownerProof: The project does not treat a separate frontend app-name placeholder as part of the normal operator contract, so the current placeholder is removable.

SSL_CERT_DOMAIN.name: SSL_CERT_DOMAIN
SSL_CERT_DOMAIN.consumerProof: TLS certificate CN defaults are documented as a derivation from APP_DOMAIN rather than a separate semantic role.
SSL_CERT_DOMAIN.ownerProof: The operator owns the public host identity, while bootstrap owns the derived certificate-domain projection.

STATE_DIR.name: STATE_DIR
STATE_DIR.consumerProof: The cleanup contract treats STATE_DIR as the canonical state root for generated runtime, backup, and rendered-path derivations.
STATE_DIR.ownerProof: STATE_DIR defaults to the repository state root and is only an advanced override, so bootstrap owns the normal value.

BT_STATE_DIR.name: BT_STATE_DIR
BT_STATE_DIR.consumerProof: Existing bootstrap, compose, and test flows still consume BT_STATE_DIR while the canonical contract moves to STATE_DIR.
BT_STATE_DIR.ownerProof: BT_STATE_DIR persists only as a compatibility projection of the canonical state root.

BT_RUNTIME_DIR.name: BT_RUNTIME_DIR
BT_RUNTIME_DIR.consumerProof: Bootstrap and shell consumers rely on BT_RUNTIME_DIR as the derived runtime directory under the state root.
BT_RUNTIME_DIR.ownerProof: Operators do not supply runtime-directory paths in the normal flow; bootstrap derives them from STATE_DIR.

GRAFANA_ADMIN_SECRET_FILE.name: GRAFANA_ADMIN_SECRET_FILE
GRAFANA_ADMIN_SECRET_FILE.consumerProof: Observability bootstrap and compose consumers treat this as a generated Grafana admin secret path under the state root.
GRAFANA_ADMIN_SECRET_FILE.ownerProof: The file path is internal runtime materialization, not an operator-owned secret input.

GRAFANA_DATASOURCES_FILE.name: GRAFANA_DATASOURCES_FILE
GRAFANA_DATASOURCES_FILE.consumerProof: Observability compose and bootstrap flows consume this rendered Grafana datasource config path as generated runtime state.
GRAFANA_DATASOURCES_FILE.ownerProof: Rendered monitoring config paths are derived from STATE_DIR rather than supplied by the operator.

PROMETHEUS_WEB_CONFIG_FILE.name: PROMETHEUS_WEB_CONFIG_FILE
PROMETHEUS_WEB_CONFIG_FILE.consumerProof: Observability compose and bootstrap flows consume this rendered Prometheus web config path as generated runtime state.
PROMETHEUS_WEB_CONFIG_FILE.ownerProof: Rendered monitoring config paths are derived under STATE_DIR and are not normal operator-facing inputs.
